title: Waszkiewicz 2025 — one 9-bar espresso extraction (individual brew 9-4)
source_authors: Waszkiewicz, R.; Myck, F.; Bialas, L.; Puciata-Mroczynska, M.; Dzikowski, M.; Szymczak, P.; Lisicki, M.
source_title: Under pressure: Poroelastic regulation of flow in espresso brewing
source_member: measurements_time_dependent/9-4.txt (raw individual brew; windowed per-shot form in formatted_measurements/debug_time_dependent.csv, filename==9-4.txt)
rights_basis: PENDING upstream clarification: Zenodo record-level license is CC-BY-4.0 (resource type Software); the archive README states the CODE is GPLv3 and does not separately declare the measurement-data license. No DATA_LICENSE file exists. The repository MANIFEST.csv records a prior CC-BY determination for this dataset, but that is project history, not an independent upstream rights grant. A rights-holder confirmation or a stable upstream data-license statement is required before this fixture is treated as redistributable.
attribution: Waszkiewicz, R. et al., "Under pressure: Poroelastic regulation of flow in espresso brewing", Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.18046315, CC BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Modified by Puckworks (see modification_notice). No upstream source code is redistributed. This does not imply endorsement by the original authors.
modification_notice: Modified from the source: model-independent medoid selection of one 9-bar brew (9-4) among 5 replicates; time aligned to the source pressure-settle index (ms->s); line pressure kPa->bar; measured channels only (time, line pressure, mass) retained; derived flow and basket pressure dropped; converted to a normalized CSV.
pressure_node: line/pump-side (source column pressure__bar = P_line, MEASURED). The basket-pressure and mass-flow-rate channels in the source are DERIVED (pump-curve subtraction; savgol-filtered gradient) and are deliberately excluded from this measured-input fixture.
time_origin: source pressure-settle alignment: t=0 at the last sample before line pressure settled within tolerance of the 9-bar reference (source format_measurements_time_dependent.py START_IDX); seconds.
missing_value_semantics: no missing values; every row has all three measured channels. Early mass values are small negative scale-tare readings, retained verbatim (not clipped).

transformations.description: source format_measurements_time_dependent.py: parse raw JSON-lines brew; align time to the 9-bar pressure-settle index; trim to MAX_TIME=100 s; time ms->s; line pressure kPa->bar (p2/100).
keep measured channels only: time__s->time_s, pressure__bar->line_pressure_bar, mass__g->mass_g; drop derived flow/basket-pressure.
sort by time_s ascending; format time_s/line_pressure_bar/mass_g to 4 decimals; UTF-8, LF line endings, trailing newline.

selection_method: medoid of the 5 nine-bar replicates (9-1..9-5): z-scored L2 distance of measured line-pressure and mass to the ensemble median on a 0-20 s / 0.1 s grid; tie-break stable filename; model-independent (no model fit). Selected 9-4.txt (distance 6.45; others 9.02/12.58/14.39/17.32).
scientific_caveats: Research-rig extraction measured to equilibrium (~140 g over ~100 s at ~9 bar), NOT a typical cafe-size espresso shot. Basket pressure and mass-flow rate are derived, not measured, and are excluded here. Only measured time, line pressure, and mass are included. No channeling, flavor, optimality, or ground-truth permeability conclusion is attached. Single rig; one identifiable brew.
